reason: Confirm producews sits at internal/producews; clarify that supervisor goroutine should not import upward (no internal/app dependency).
Service struct + mu lock conventions: state mutators acquire mu.Lock/Unlock, no blocking I/O inside locks. Supervisor must Sleep / Listen / Serve outside the lock.

Error wrapping patterns and sentinel-vs-text discipline. Stop signal must be a typed channel / context cancellation, never a stringly-typed sentinel; retry-exhaustion message goes into wsState.LastError, not into a returned error chain.
Decides log level per retry attempt (info) and on exhaustion (warning). Defines structured fields convention if any emit/log is added inside producews.
Naive UI n-alert usage pattern; ProducePage.vue will add an alert mirroring the existing queueState.last_error alert.
If the new ws_state error display uses i18n, only zh-CN.json may be edited; en-US.json is user-maintained.
Before introducing a backoff sequence constant, check whether existing helpers (e.g., ffmpegprofile.BuildRetryChain) can be reused — confirmed no, but the discipline applies.

Supervisor mutates wsState which propagates Service mu → emit callback → wruntime.EventsEmit → frontend produce_ws_state_changed → useProducePage.ts → ProducePage.vue alert. Verify the chain end-to-end during implementation.

Source of truth for this task: scope (Option C: 5 retries, no manual button), Start() contract (sync first attempt + async supervisor), backoff sequence, and the architectural clarification that the WS client is the HLAE plugin DLL injected into CS2 (not HLAE process itself).
